Leo Tyrell
the Lazy
Leo Tyrell, by Grandkhan ©
Aliases
  • Lazy Leo[1]
  • Leo the Lazy[2]
Title Novice[1]
Allegiances
  • House Tyrell
  • The Citadel
Culture Reach
Born Reach[3]
Father Moryn Tyrell
Books
  • A Storm of Swords (appendix)
  • A Feast for Crows (appears)

Leo Tyrell, known as Lazy Leo, is a member of House Tyrell, a first cousin of Mace Tyrell, the Lord of Highgarden.[2] He is the second son of Ser Moryn Tyrell, the commander of Oldtown's City Watch.[4] Leo is a novice at the Citadel.[2]

Contents

  • 1 Appearance and Character
  • 2 History
  • 3 Recent Events
    • 3.1 A Feast for Crows
  • 4 Quotes by Leo
  • 5 Quotes about Leo
  • 6 Family
  • 7 References

Appearance and Character[ ]

See also: Images of Leo Tyrell

Leo has sly hazel eyes and ash-blond hair that falls over one eye,[1][5] coloring that stands out from the traditional brown eyes and brown hair of the Tyrells. He is handsome, with pale skin.[5] Leo has a soft, sly voice.[1]

Leo dresses in satin striped in the Tyrell colors of green and gold. He has worn a black silk half cape, pinned to his shoulder by a jade rose,[1] and a half cape striped in green and gold.[5]

The arrogant and malicious Leo often gives mocking nicknames to his acquaintances, and speaks unpleasantly of Dornishmen and Summer Islanders. He is disliked by his fellow students. Leo sometimes gambles at the Checkered Hazard, to the point of losing all his money and having to beg for drinks from acquaintances. He has been trained to arms, and is known to be deadly with a bravo's blade and dagger.[1]

History[ ]

Leo was acquainted with Samwell Tarly during their childhood. Sam does not have fond memories of their interactions.[5]

At some point in their time at the Citadel, Leo nicknamed Alleras "the Sphinx", as he was "a bit of this, a bit of that", like a sphinx is made of multiple creatures. Their fellow students picked up on the nickname,[1] and Alleras himself even uses it.[5] However, Leo's nickname of "Hopfrog" for Mollander (for his clubfoot) is detested.[1]

Recent Events[ ]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

Leo had been confined to the Citadel for some unspecified offense, but leaves three days early to gamble and drink in the inns of Oldtown. He arrives at the Quill and Tankard, where Alleras, Roone, Armen, Mollander, and Pate have been celebrating Alleras's new link and talking about dragons. Leo confirms that the rumors of the sailors are true, that Daenerys Targaryen is alive and has hatched three living dragons. He also reveals that there is a glass candle burning in the chambers of Archmaester Marwyn. As the others leave the inn, he threatens to take the virginity of Rosey, the girl that Pate has fallen for, but Alleras reassures Pate that if Leo cannot afford to buy wine, he cannot afford a gold dragon to pay Rosey's mother.[1]

Samwell Tarly, newly arrived at the Citadel, recognizes Leo outside the chambers of Marwyn at the Ravenry. Leo is staring intently into a candle flame, and when Alleras asks him if he is searching for his destiny or his death, Leo blinks and replies, "Naked women." Sam is intimidated by Leo, remembering him from their youth, but nevertheless tells him of his deeds in the Night's Watch and that he is now called "Sam the Slayer". Leo laughs, but has no chance to reply as Sam enters Marwyn's chambers.[5]

Quotes by Leo[ ]

Armen: I had understood that you were still confined to the Citadel for...
Leo: ...three more days. Perestan says the world is forty thousand years old. Mollos says five hundred thousand. What are three days, I ask you?[1]

—Armen and Leo

Leo: I understand you won your copper link. I'll drink to that.
Alleras: I only buy for friends.[1]

—Alleras and Leo

Alleras: You shame your House with every word you say. You shame the Citadel by being one of us.
Leo: I know. So buy me some wine, that I might drown my shame.[1]

—Alleras and Leo

Mollander: I would tear your tongue out by the roots.
Leo: Truly? Then how would I tell you about the dragons?[1]

—Mollander and Leo

Dragons and darker things. The grey sheep have closed their eyes, but the mastiff sees the truth. Old powers awaken. Shadows stir. An age of wonder and terror will soon be upon us, an age for gods and heroes.[1]

—Leo, to Alleras

Armen: We've drunk enough. Morn will be upon us sooner than we'd like, and Archmaester Ebrose will be speaking on the properties of urine. Those who mean to forge a silver link would do well not to miss his talk.

Leo: Far be it from me to keep you from the piss tasting. Myself, I prefer the taste of Arbor gold.

Mollander: If the choice is piss or you, I'll drink piss.[1]

—Armen, Leo, and Mollander

The Citadel is not what it was. They will take anything these days. Dusky dogs and Dornishmen, pig boys, cripples, cretins, and now a black-clad whale. And here I thought leviathans were grey.[5]

—Leo, to Samwell Tarly

Quotes about Leo[ ]

He has a mocking name for everyone.[1]

—thoughts of Pate

Leo had two names where Pate had only one, and his second was Tyrell.[1]

—thoughts of Pate
